questions.question: What is the purpose of the `np.mean()` function in Python?
options: A) To calculate the median of a dataset
B) To calculate the mean of a dataset
C) To calculate the standard deviation of a dataset
D) To calculate the variance of a dataset
questions.explanation: np.mean() calculates the arithmetic mean of the elements in a given array.

questions.question: What is the difference between `np.array()` and `list()` in Python?
options: A) np.array() is faster than list()
B) np.array() is slower than list()
C) np.array() creates a two-dimensional array while list() creates a one-dimensional list
D) np.array() creates a one-dimensional array while list() creates a two-dimensional list
questions.explanation: np.array() creates a two-dimensional array while list() creates a one-dimensional list.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `pandas.to_csv()` function in Python?
options: A) To read a CSV file into a pandas DataFrame
B) To write a pandas DataFrame to a CSV file
C) To merge two pandas DataFrames
D) To group a pandas DataFrame by a column
questions.explanation: pandas.to_csv() writes a pandas DataFrame to a CSV file.

questions.question: What is the difference between `np.sort()` and `sorted()` in Python?
options: A) np.sort() sorts a one-dimensional array while sorted() sorts a list
B) np.sort() sorts a list while sorted() sorts a one-dimensional array
C) np.sort() sorts a pandas DataFrame while sorted() sorts a dictionary
D) np.sort() sorts a dictionary while sorted() sorts a pandas DataFrame
questions.explanation: np.sort() sorts a one-dimensional array while sorted() sorts a list.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `matplotlib.pyplot.plot()` function in Python?
options: A) To create a scatter plot
B) To create a line plot
C) To create a bar chart
D) To create a histogram
